title: Vivian
summary: The Lady of the Lake, also known as Vivian or Nimue, according to legend, she is most famous for giving Arthur the magical sword Excalibur, for fostering Sir Lancelot after his father's death and for imprisoning Merlin in a Tower or Tree or at times; a Cave.

title: Lu Dongbin
summary: Lü Dongbin is a Chinese immortal revered as a hero of marvelous and infinite wisdom by Taoists. He was born during the Tang Dynasty in the 8th century and converted to Taoism by Zhongli Quan. Lü Dongbin is the most widely known of the Eight Immortals and hence considered by some to be the informal leader. His real name is Lü Yán (Chinese: 呂巖 or 呂岩). Dòngbīn is his courtesy name. Dòngbīn literally means 'cave visitor'.

title: Zhongli Quan
summary: Work in progress.

title: He Xiangu
summary: Work in progress.

title: Su Daji
summary: The person closest to Sādàn (“撒旦, Satan”) within Chinese History and Folklore. No, It can only be said that she is truly the devil herself. A nation-toppling beauty who lives only for her own amusement, clothing herself in the image of a beautiful consort. Opening herself to be taken like she’s a pure maiden only to stomp over one’s heart as effortlessly as humans stomp over nature. She seems to adore humans in her own way, likening herself to their fangirl, loving every twisted idea—every twisted desire—every indulgence spawned because of them. That is the humanity she adores and sees, no more and no less.

title: Xu Fu
summary: Work in progress.

title: Yue Nü
summary: Yue Nü is the given title of a nameless woman who appeared during King Goujian of Yue’s  reign. Said to have been raised within a Bamboo forest by a White Ape, she had mysteriously appeared during the War of Wu when both were attempting to recruit a Swordmaster to train their armies. It is said that Yue Nü would then reveal the philosophy of the Way of the Sword to King Goujian, who then gave her the title of Yue Nü as a form of an honorary title for the Nameless Swordmaster.

title: Selene
summary: Work in progress.

title: Izanami-No-Mikoto
summary: Work in progress.

title: Medea
summary: The daughter of Aeëtes, the king of Colchis from Greek mythology, owner of the golden fleece. She is a bonafide princess, but she is also a witch from the Age of the Gods, taught by the Moon Goddess Hekate.

title: Ozymandias
summary: A wise ruler and exemplary statesman who, while fighting the Hittites, eventually made peace and brought about a thriving prosperity to ancient Egypt by means of 'cultural exchange and interaction'. He is in particular a valorous general, and a man who had married the world's most beautiful women and fathered over a hundred children. Even in the current era, he is famous for leaving behind a large number of various colossal constructions. He loved the masses just like Osiris would, and he was greatly loved by the people in return. The very golden age of ancient Egypt is during his longstanding reign of 7X years.

title: Odin
summary: Rework in progress.

title: Lü Zhi
summary: Better known as Lǚ Hòu or Empress Lü, she is the 'First Empress' and a legendary founding figure of China known as the Empress Gao Of Han. She was once known as Lü Zhi, a gentle and graceful noble lady, who’d fallen for the “Future Red Emperor.”

title: Liang Hongyu
summary: Work in progress.

title: Seshat
summary: Work in progress.

title: Hekate
summary: One of the Trimorphis Manifestations of Hekate—with Brimo (The Terrifying), otherwise known as Hekate-Kardiodiatos and Anassa Eneroi—as the primary face. Hekate is a Goddess who dwells within the Abyss, holding the Key at the Boundary, and seems to have crossed over in that respect to manifest as a Servant of the Assassin-Class.

title: Hildegard Von Bingen
summary: Work in progress.

title: Nike
summary: Watcher of Victory - The Great Goddess Nike.

title: Vanadís Freyja
summary: Work in progress.

title: Susanoo-No-Mikoto
summary: Work in progress.

title: Astrid Diamantopoulou
summary: Astrid Diamantopoulou, also known as Blue Stargazer, is the acting head of the Diamantis Family and supporting heroine of Fate/Revelation. She appears as the protagonist of Fate/Astra in her youth. She once sought to fulfill the Grand Order of her Family as was expected of her, but she came to develop her own Grand Order, and cast her gaze on the starry seas.

title: Celeste Turgard
summary: An optimist above all else, but also a brilliant idiot. A whimsical and immature person whose temperament is unfit for both an Enforcer of the Thule, and a Magus of supposed unfathomable genius. A person who lacks the proper intelligence and common sense needed to live as an academic magus, but could be said to be more fit for the olden times where devout faith was all but common sense — where simply doing the supernatural — was natural. True to her lacking academics, she has a very loose grasp on things like history — politics — and even social matters. It’s not to say she is a social idiot, but rather, she is inherently lacking in applied charisma — and that charismatic aura is only a byproduct of the supernatural rather than her own talents.

title: Mei Nieyin
summary: Eastern Magus of Fiends (Daemons). A person similar in nature to Kirei Kotomine, but vastly different in that rather than possessing a common sense inherently leaning toward the immoral - unable to grasp the importance of 'good' - she instead possesses the inability to understand morality at all, a common sense completely alien to the rest of man since the moment of her birth — a sinner from the moment of her existence long before she became a mass of curses and fiends.

title: Aris Magentin
summary: Not the true name of the Golden haired man. Perhaps not even his true face. He proclaims himself to be of a different time, inconsistently. His name is an anagram but also a possible lie. He carries the mysteries of the east, their crest, but the swordsmanship and style of the west — the visage of an old noble — and the way of life some modern man or future man. Unusual in every sense of the word, no telling where lies begin and where truths come about with this man. A walking puzzle to be deciphered.

title: Sibylle Itud-dara
summary: Work in progress.

title: Metatron
summary: Work in progress.

title: Melpomeni Cosmo Othonos
summary: Work in progress.

title: Remissus Punitor
summary: One who claims himself as a specialist in binding arts and in matters of faith. An oddball like the rest of the original Burial Agency, but a far cry from Narbareck. It is said that the scripture he carries, that of binding, is an 'Angel' from the depths of the Church.

title: Oriane Nouvel
summary: Work in progress.

title: Hina Hasegawa
summary: The Shadow of Arasaka is somebody that most people don’t agree on the existence of. From time to time, the Shadow will be brought up as a horror story that spread around to discourage action against Arasaka. Others believe it to just be a story based on a long past person. The truth is uncertain, what is certain is that the Shadow of Arasaka is only a story that can be told in full by those sitting at the top of their respective Corporations.

title: Sophia Tokunaga
summary: A Freelance Media often known under the handle of “Harmony” and recognized by her special insignia. She is often the source behind big stories exposing the misdeeds of corpos yet is rarely the one running the story herself. Often selling off stories to media outlets who’d find benefit in running stories that negatively impact the reputation of rival corpos for little pay. She rather asks for the condition of being given explicit credit as a source over high pay for those stories..

title: 'Kiyoko'
summary: Shizumi Kiyoko (静海潔子) is an original character within the story of [✞]//: 𝕄𝔢𝔱𝔞𝔫𝔬𝔦𝔞 . She is a Saint acting on her own without official support from any faction. Widely renowned as a “Peerless Sword” and an “Unworthy Saint,” she is known to wander the streets of Nocturne City. To those following the Path of Sin, she either offers mercy or death as far as rumors go, being considered savage in behavior and cruelty. Demons and the like tend to claim she hunts them actively, when in truth, she has no desire and can be considered lost on her journey…wandering without purpose. She does seem to be praised often by people for “saving them.”

title: 'Soo-Mi'
summary: Soo-Mi is a Messenger and Diplomat under Kyo’s employ, but in truth, she mostly acts as a Mediator amongst Lycans in spite of her not being a Werewolf. As one of three Nature Spirits, one who is representative of “Dreams,” she is unusually empathetic and carefree. At the same time, it is accurate to describe her as “disturbing” and contradictorily incompatible and compatible with Life outside of other Lycans.

title: Orlok D. Erzsébet
summary: Orlok D. Erzsébet is an original character within the story of Dreams Adrift. She is a B+ ranking Driftwood Pirate whose fixated on the idea of permanence. Yet even with her fixation on permanence, she doesn’t seem to care about her own life—as if dying isn’t a fear she has. An action only taken for it is rational to seek permanence. To achieve permanence and thus security. An extreme rational egoist to her core that is willing to commit countless atrocities for as long as it has purpose. Many believe her goal to be defying the natural order and attaining “Divinity” for immortality…yet in truth—the only way to attain absolute Divinity is when you are the only divinity left on the board.

title: Cordelia
summary: The eternal ray of sunlight that once shone all across the lands, once known as a pure and kind Faeri even amongst her own kind. One who held no judgement or hatred in her heart toward any, even lending her light to the likes of the Daemon-Kind. With her faith in hand, many saw her as the perfect ideal of morality. One whose light was to be followed as an example. Yet, she who wished to heal the world and aid all the land, was one burdened with great pain and sorrow...A fallen angel.

title: Marie-Éléonore Adélaïde De Clermont-Tonnerre
summary: Marie-Éléonore Adélaïde De Clermont-Tonnerre (“The Bitter Light of a Stolen Grace, a High Beacon that hides the Storm of the Grave.”) is a haematophage of the Vampiric Species, proclaiming herself an eloquent noble lady and veiling herself in lavish attires—playing a variety of music and practicing what she describes as a “civilized way of life.” All in hopes of being able to maintain a sense of humanity; to separate herself from that of a wild haematophagic beast, and “change.”

title: Vera
summary: Vera Propitia can be read as “The Grace Of yielding Law” or more symbolically as “The Hollow Benediction.” Propitia is her family name, and Vera was her mother’s wish for her. To be faithful and true to herself. It is an irony for her, whose very name can be read to reflect her true self—contradictory to her mother’s wishes—an empty husk of a person. Somebody who lives only by the ideal they were shaped by and for, that of Magic, and nothing more. Or perhaps she was empty from the moment she was born.
